title: Multi-timeframe confirmation: use 1h trend to filter 5m entries
description: Strategy signals on 5m candles are noisy. A 1h uptrend with a 5m pullback is a much better entry than a 5m signal against the 1h trend. Add higher-timeframe trend filtering.
design:
- Add 1h candle aggregation to CandleSeries (or fetch from DB if available)
- Calculate 1h EMA trend (fast=9, slow=21) as trend filter
- Only allow LONG entries when 1h trend is bullish (fast > slow)
- Only allow SHORT entries when 1h trend is bearish (fast < slow)
- Store 1h trend state in strategy, update every 12 candles (1 hour on 5m)
- Can be done by aggregating 5m candles into 1h on the fly
- Alternative: add a resample method to CandleSeries
acceptance_criteria:
- Strategy checks higher-timeframe trend before signaling
- Diagnostic shows fewer but better trades (higher win rate)
- Trades against 1h trend are filtered out
- Compare results with/without MTF filter

title: Trailing stops: lock in profits as price moves in our favor
description: Currently positions use fixed take-profit targets. When price moves 2% in our favor then reverses, we give back all gains. Trailing stops would lock in partial profits.
design:
- Implement trailing stop in Position model: track highest price since entry for longs
- Trail stop at ATR distance behind highest price
- Only activate trailing after price moves 1x ATR in our favor (breakeven activation)
- Update _update_positions_with_candle to check trailing stop using candle H/L
- Config params: trailing_activation_atr=1.0, trailing_distance_atr=2.0
acceptance_criteria:
- Position model has trailing stop fields and update logic
- Backtest engine checks trailing stops alongside regular SL/TP
- Diagnostic shows trades held longer with less giveback
- Win rate increases compared to fixed TP only

title: Better entry timing: wait for pullback within trend
description: Momentum strategy enters as soon as 3/5 indicators agree, which often means entering at the top of a move. This leads to immediate stop losses. Add pullback detection so we enter on dips within a trend.
design:
- Add pullback detector: when trend is LONG (EMA fast > slow, RSI > 50), wait for price to dip toward the fast EMA before entering
- Add RSI divergence check: if RSI is dropping while price is rising, skip entry (momentum weakening)
- Require entry candle to be bullish (close > open) for longs, bearish for shorts
- Consider using previous candle low as natural entry level
acceptance_criteria:
- Strategy has pullback/patience mechanism
- Diagnostic backtest shows reduced trade count but higher win rate
- Fewer stop_loss exits, more take_profit exits
